mission.title: Install and Configure Kitops on Kubernetes
mission.description: KitOps is an open source DevOps tool for packaging and versioning AI/ML models, datasets, code, and configuration into an OCI Artifact. Installing KitOps allows you to manage your AI/ML projects efficiently and securely, leveraging the power of the CNCF ecosystem.

steps.title: Install Kit CLI
steps.description:
Install the Kit CLI using the install script:
```bash
# macOS / Linux
curl -fsSL https://kitops.org/install.sh | bash

# Or via Homebrew
brew install kitops-ml/kitops/kit
```
See https://kitops.org/docs/cli/installation/ for all platforms.

steps.title: Create a Kitfile
steps.description:
Create a `Kitfile` to define your ModelKit:
```yaml
manifestVersion: 1.0.0
package:
  name: my-model
  version: 0.1.0
model:
  name: my-model
  path: ./model
  framework: onnx
```

steps.title: Pack and push a ModelKit
steps.description:
Pack your project and push to an OCI registry:
```bash
kit pack . -t myregistry/my-model:v1
kit push myregistry/my-model:v1
```

resolution.summary: KitOps Kit CLI is installed and can package, version, and distribute AI/ML projects as ModelKits using OCI registries.

uninstall.title: Remove Kit CLI
uninstall.description:
```bash
# If installed via script
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/kit

# If installed via Homebrew
brew uninstall kit
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kit CLI
upgrade.description:
```bash
# Via Homebrew
brew upgrade kit

# Or via install script
curl -fsSL https://kitops.org/install.sh | bash
```

troubleshooting.title: Registry authentication errors
troubleshooting.description:
Log in to your OCI registry before pushing:
```bash
kit login myregistry.io
```

troubleshooting.title: Kitfile validation errors
troubleshooting.description: Ensure your Kitfile follows the specification at https://kitops.org/docs/kitfile/kf-overview/

troubleshooting.title: Large model files slow to push
troubleshooting.description: KitOps uses OCI layer deduplication. Subsequent pushes of the same model are faster as unchanged layers are cached.

prerequisites.description: No Kubernetes cluster required. Kit is a standalone CLI tool for ML model packaging.
